title: File System Tools
description: JSON Schema definitions for file system tools available to the ideas-brillantes assistant. Provides read/write access to the local Linux file system, directory listing, file movement, deletion, and search.

tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read the full text content of a file from the local file system.

path.description: Absolute or relative path to the file to read (e.g. '/home/user/notes.txt').

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Write or update a file on the local file system.

path.description: Absolute or relative path where the file should be written.

content.description: Text content to write into the file.

mode.description: 'overwrite' replaces the file (default), 'append' adds content to the end, 'create_new' fails if the file already exists.

tools.name: list_dir
tools.description: List the contents of a directory, optionally including all subdirectories.

path.description: Absolute or relative path to the directory to list.

recursive.description: If true, list all files and folders in subdirectories as well.

tools.name: move_file
tools.description: Move or rename a file or directory from one path to another.

src.description: Source path of the file or directory to move.

dst.description: Destination path (including the new filename if renaming).

tools.name: delete_file
tools.description: Permanently delete a file or directory. Requires explicit confirmation to prevent accidental deletion.

path.description: Absolute or relative path to the file or directory to delete.

confirm.description: Must be set to true to confirm that deletion is intentional. The call is rejected if false or omitted.

tools.name: search_files
tools.description: Search for files matching a query string in their name or content within a directory.

query.description: Search term or regex pattern to match against file names or contents.

path.description: Optional root directory to search in. Defaults to the current working directory.

type.description: Whether to match against file names, file contents, or both. Defaults to 'name'.

tools.name: create_dir
tools.description: Create a new directory (and any missing parent directories).

path.description: Absolute or relative path of the directory to create. Intermediate directories are created automatically.
